files.SKILL.md:
---
name: agent-working-notes
description: Practical working notes for terminal agents. Identify deliverable and hard constraints first. Read referenced sources before writing. Anchor output early. Copy stateful resources before probing. Implement incrementally and verify each step. Change mechanism after repeated structural failure. Reproduce spec literals exactly. Read implementations before predicting behavior. Confirm completion by reading artifacts on disk.
---

# Agent Working Notes

Disk artifacts are the answer. Everything else is
intermediate work.

## Turn discipline

Start every task by identifying the deliverable:
its exact path, format, and required behavior. Read
the task instruction completely, then read every
source file the task references before writing
anything.

Components behave according to their actual code,
not their category name. Read the implementation
before predicting behavior.

Create the output file with stub content immediately.
Update it in place throughout the task.

Before operating on a resource that may change when
opened or read, copy it to a working location and
operate on the copy.

Implement once you have a plausible solution, then
validate with checks you control: compile, run, read
back, or probe behavior. Fix what the failure names
literally. Repeat.

Three failures with the same structure means the
structure is wrong. Diagnose the root cause and
change approach.

One paragraph of planning, then action.

## Tools

Shell, Python, and file I/O. Use only packages
already available in the environment and the standard
library. No speculative imports.

## Spec compliance

Literals in the task description are hard constraints.
Reproduce dates, paths, names, and format characters
exactly.

Pinned dates must appear as literal values in code,
not as runtime clock expressions.

When the task provides sample output, match it
character for character: separators, quoting, spacing,
and trailing newlines.

State boundary conventions explicitly when intervals
or ranges matter.

## Read implementations before using them

When output must satisfy an existing component, read
its implementation first. The implementation defines
what is accepted and rejected.

When data flows through multiple components, inspect
the intermediate result from the upstream step before
reasoning about what the downstream step will do.

## Values from the environment

When the task requires values from the workspace,
read them from the actual sources present. Search
broadly — different paths, extensions, and
representations — before concluding a value is absent.
Do not substitute inference or prior knowledge for a
required read.

## Quality discipline

Fix the smallest unit that resolves the failure. Use
stable time sources for elapsed-time measurement.
Validate at trust boundaries. Comments only for
non-obvious reasoning.

When writing content with shell-special characters,
use direct file tools rather than shell interpolation,
then read the file back to confirm.

## Before stopping

Read the artifact on disk. If it diverges from your
current understanding, update it. Completion is
confirmed by what exists on disk, not by recollection
of actions taken.
